insights: North America accounts for 34% of total expenses ($6.7M) despite representing only 19.8% of records, reflecting a systematic 106% average premium per record across all categories — not attributable to category mix, department concentration, or timing anomalies.

Department is the dominant driver of both expense levels ($24,864 range across departments) and processing time (55.7-day range), with Customer Support averaging $54,895 per record and Product Management taking 55.7 days on average — far exceeding the influence of priority or assignment group.

The North America cost premium is structurally consistent over time, maintaining an average 2.12x ratio to non-NA regions across all months (range: 1.69x–2.52x), confirming it is embedded in regional operational norms rather than seasonal or transient factors.
Processing time shows only weak correlation with expense amounts (Pearson r = 0.11), indicating that improving workflow efficiency is unlikely to meaningfully reduce overall spending levels.
IT department exhibits 0.0 days processed_time across all 74 records — a data integrity anomaly requiring validation, as it distorts processing-time benchmarks and masks actual workflow behavior.

Category distribution is balanced overall (Services 29.4%, Miscellaneous 25.5%, Travel 23.6%, Assets 21.5%) and stable over time, confirming no single category drives temporal or regional spending variation.

Top 5 location-category combinations account for ~40% of total spend, with North America appearing in 4 of the top 5 — highlighting geographic-category co-concentration as a key lever for targeted budget governance.

Monthly spending is highly stable (avg $1.52M, std $268K), with only September and October 2023 showing statistically anomalous spikes (z = 2.11 and 1.74), suggesting isolated operational events rather than systemic trend shifts.
summary: The analysis reveals that geographic location — specifically North America — is the strongest structural driver of expense variation, contributing 34% of total spend with a consistent 106% per-record premium across all categories and time periods, pointing to embedded regional cost norms rather than tactical or temporal causes. Department is the second most influential factor, driving both the highest expense levels (Customer Support at $54,895) and longest processing times (Product Management at 55.7 days), while priority and assignment group have negligible impact. Critically, processing time and expense amounts are weakly correlated (r = 0.11), indicating that workflow efficiency initiatives alone will not materially reduce costs. Given this, budget allocation should prioritize regional cost benchmarking and department-level spend governance, especially for North America and high-spend departments, while workflow improvements should focus first on validating and diagnosing the IT department’s 0.0-day processing-time anomaly before broader process redesign.
